Alice Hunt Welles
(3 April 1543 – 15 July 1615)

Their children born/baptized in Stourton, Warwickshire:

Jane Welles baptized April 24, 1569.

zh.lifeSummary: Alice Hunt於1543年4月3日在英國瓦維其可爾斯圖頓受洗。她於1565年與Robert Welles在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾阿斯頓 克林頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和8個女兒的父母。她去世於1615年7月5日在她的家鄉享年72歲，埋葬在英國瓦維其可爾傑林頓。

nameMeanings.name: Hunt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southwestern): occupational name for a hunter, from Middle English hunte ‘hunter, huntsman’ (Old English hunta). The term was used not only of the hunting on horseback of game such as stags and wild boars, which in the Middle Ages was a pursuit restricted to the ranks of the nobility, but also to much humbler forms of pursuit such as bird catching and poaching for food. The word seems also to have been used as an Old English personal name and to have survived into the Middle Ages as an occasional personal name. Compare Huntington and Huntley .
Irish: adopted for various Irish surnames containing or thought to contain the Gaelic element fiadhach ‘hunt’; for example Ó Fiaich (see Fee ) and Ó Fiachna (see Fenton ).
Possibly an Americanized form of German   Hundt  .

variants.name: Hunter
meanings: Scottish and northern English: occupational name from Middle English hunter(e) ‘huntsman’ (see Hunt ).
Irish (Antrim and Derry): adopted for Gaelic Ó Fiaich (see Fee ) due to confusion with the word fiadhach ‘hunt’.
History: A Scottish family of this name (see 1 above) has been established at Hunterston (Ayrshire) since the 13th century.

variants.name: Huntley
meanings: English: habitational name from Huntley in Gloucestershire, from Old English hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’ (see Hunt ) + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Occasionally the name may arise from Huntley in Staffordshire, of the same etymology, but there is no medieval evidence that this gave rise to a surname.

Scottish: habitational name from a lost place called Huntlie in Berwickshire (Borders), with the same etymology as in 1 above. Huntly in Aberdeenshire was named for a medieval Earl of Huntly who took his title from the Borders placename, and is not the source of the surname.

English: occasionally perhaps a habitational name from Huntley in Preston on Wye (Herefordshire), first recorded in 1290 as a surname, (de) Huntelaw. The placename derives from Old English hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’ + hlāw ‘mound, hill’.

variants.name: Fenton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places, in Yorkshire, Lincolnshire, Northumberland, Staffordshire, and Nottinghamshire, so called from Old English fenn ‘marsh, fen’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

Irish: English surname adopted by bearers of Gaelic Ó Fionnachta or Ó Fiachna ‘descendant of Fiachna’, an old personal name Anglicized as Feighney and sometimes mistranslated as Hunt (see Fee 1 and Finnerty ).
Scottish: habitational name from Fenton in East Lothian.

variants.name: Huntington
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places called Huntington, most of which are named with Old English huntena, the genitive plural form of hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’, + dūn ‘hill’ or tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Some, however, may have Old English hunting ‘hunting’ as the initial element, while others may derive from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan), perhaps with Old English connective -ing-, + tūn. The surname may also arise from Huntingdon (Huntingdonshire), which derives from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan) or Old English hunta (genitive huntan) + dūn.

History: A prominent American family of this name were founded by Simon Huntington, who himself never saw the New World, for he died in 1633 on the voyage to Boston, where his widow settled with her children. Their descendants include Jabez Huntington (1719–86), a wealthy West Indies trader, and Samuel Huntington (1731–96), who was one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. Collis Potter Huntington (1821–1900) was an American railway magnate. Beginning with little education or money, he made a huge fortune, some of which he left to his nephew, Henry Huntington (1850–1927), who used the money to establish the Huntington library and art gallery in CA.

variants.name: Hunte
meanings: English: variant of   Hunt  . This form of the surname is common in the West Indies (mainly Barbados and Trinidad and Tobago) and Guyana.
North German: habitational name from the Hunte river in Lower Saxony.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: Alice Hunt was christened on 3 April 1543, in Stourton, Warwickshire, England. She married Robert Welles in 1565, in Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 8 daughters. She died on 5 July 1615, in her hometown, at the age of 72, and was buried in Cherington, Warwickshire, England.

Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 8 filles. Elle est décédée le 5 juillet 1615, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterrée à Cherington, Warwickshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Huntは1543年4月3日にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアストウルトンで洗礼を受けた。1565年にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアアストン クリントンでRobert Wellesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼女は1615年7月5日に故郷で72歳で死去し，イギリスワーウィクシュアイアチェリントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Hunt(은)는 1543년 4월 3일 영국 워윅실르 스토우르턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1565년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 애스턴 클린턴에서 Robert Welles(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1615년 7월 5일 그녀의 고향에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워윅실르 처링턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Alice Hunt была крещена 3 апреля 1543 г. в местности стоуртон, варвиккшир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Welles в 1565 г. в местности астон клинтон, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 8 дочерей. Она умерла 5 июля 1615 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 72 лет и была похоронена в местности чирингтон, варвиккшир, Англия.
